behavioural_scope.notes: change_type=MIXED correct. Sprint modifies frontend composition/conditional rendering/progressive disclosure. No Intelligence Core touched. Behavioural surface: (1) possible change to advancedOpen default state at page.tsx:129, (2) conditional bridge copy rendering, (3) ordering/emphasis of existing sub-elements. All bounded to frontend rendering layer only. No backend contracts change.

regression_targets.notes: FE-R8 regression: InterpretationPatternsSection.test.tsx covers IDL rendering. FE-R8A regression: analysisResult.test.ts:188-202 covers payload mapping including primary_driver_system_id. Both must pass after sprint changes.

stop_conditions.notes: Five STOP conditions present and adequate. Main quality problems (mechanically stitched sections, competing primary framings, hidden narrative) are frontend composition problems, not backend copy quality issues. No backend contract changes needed. Deterministic reasoning outputs do not need to change. Sprint remains bounded to frontend composition changes.

no_op_risk.notes: Concrete defects confirmed: static hero framing, mechanically concatenated body overview, repeated tokens, competing 'primary' framings in InsightPanel and ClusterSummary, disconnected card stack, hidden narrative behind Advanced analysis accordion (advancedOpen defaults false at page.tsx:129). Sprint has legitimate and non-trivial work.

forbidden_files.notes: No .codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md in scope. No backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/ files in scope. No control-plane scripts in scope.

duplicate_authority.notes: Each governed asset consumed from single source. No duplicate authority sources introduced or at risk of introduction. Rule 6 (preserve existing governed authority) confirmed adequate — no taxonomy or interpretation logic may move into frontend composition code.
